title: Cross-Namespace Routing via ReferenceGrant
description:
## Cross-Namespace Routing via ReferenceGrant

For security reasons, Gateway API disallows cross-namespace resource references by default. To enable cross-namespace references (such as an `HTTPRoute` routing to a backend `Service` in a different namespace, or a `Gateway` referencing a `Secret` in a different namespace), a `ReferenceGrant` must be created in the target namespace to explicitly permit the access.

**Your task:**

Configure cross-namespace routing from the `gateway-infra` namespace to the `gateway-apps` namespace:
1. Create an `HTTPRoute` named `cross-ns-route` in the `gateway-infra` namespace that:
   - Attaches to the parent Gateway `external-gateway` in the `gateway-infra` namespace.
   - Routes all traffic (path prefix `/`) to the backend service `backend-service` in the `gateway-apps` namespace on port `80`.
2. Create a `ReferenceGrant` named `allow-cross-ns` in the `gateway-apps` namespace that authorizes HTTPRoutes from the `gateway-infra` namespace to reference Services in the `gateway-apps` namespace.

```bash
# Verify your progress:
kubectl get referencegrant allow-cross-ns -n gateway-apps -o yaml
kubectl get httproute cross-ns-route -n gateway-infra -o yaml
```

hints.title: Cross-namespace HTTPRoute definition
hints.body: The HTTPRoute should reside in gateway-infra. Under spec.rules[].backendRefs[0], specify namespace: gateway-apps alongside the service name and port.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: HTTPRoute
metadata:
  name: cross-ns-route
  namespace: gateway-infra
spec:
  parentRefs:
  - name: external-gateway
  rules:
  - matches:
    - path:
        type: PathPrefix
        value: /
    backendRefs:
    - name: backend-service
      namespace: gateway-apps
      port: 80
EOF

hints.title: ReferenceGrant definition
hints.body: The ReferenceGrant must reside in the target namespace (gateway-apps). It defines spec.from (describing who wants to reference: Group gateway.networking.k8s.io, Kind HTTPRoute, Namespace gateway-infra) and spec.to (describing what resource is referenced: Group "" (core), Kind Service). Note: ReferenceGrant API version is gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1beta1.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: ReferenceGrant
metadata:
  name: allow-cross-ns
  namespace: gateway-apps
spec:
  from:
  - group: gateway.networking.k8s.io
    kind: HTTPRoute
    namespace: gateway-infra
  to:
  - group: ""
    kind: Service
EOF

setup_commands.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: GatewayClass
metadata:
  name: shared-gateway-class
spec:
  controllerName: example.com/gateway-controller
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: external-gateway
  namespace: gateway-infra
spec:
  gatewayClassName: shared-gateway-class
  listeners:
  - name: http-listener
    port: 80
    protocol: HTTP
    allowedRoutes:
      namespaces:
        from: All
EOF
kubectl create deployment backend-service --image=nginx:alpine -n gateway-apps 2>/dev/null || true
kubectl expose deployment backend-service --port=80 -n gateway-apps 2>/dev/null || true

validation.description: Check that the HTTPRoute in gateway-infra and ReferenceGrant in gateway-apps are configured correctly

commands.description: HTTPRoute cross-ns-route exists in gateway-infra namespace
commands.command: kubectl get httproute cross-ns-route -n gateway-infra -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'

commands.description: ReferenceGrant allow-cross-ns exists in gateway-apps namespace
commands.command: kubectl get referencegrant allow-cross-ns -n gateway-apps -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get referencegrant allow-cross-ns -n gateway-apps -o jsonpath='{.spec.from[0].group}'
kubectl get referencegrant allow-cross-ns -n gateway-apps -o jsonpath='{.spec.from[0].kind}'
kubectl get referencegrant allow-cross-ns -n gateway-apps -o jsonpath='{.spec.from[0].namespace}'
kubectl get referencegrant allow-cross-ns -n gateway-apps -o jsonpath='{.spec.to[0].kind}'
